Title: Authoring shaders in Bleepforge
Summary: The shaders surface lets you browse, edit, and live-preview every .gdshader in your Godot project. File-based — no .tres round-trip, no JSON cache.
Body:
## What the surface is

[Shaders](/shaders) is Bleepforge's tenth surface and the second file-based one (after [Assets](/assets)). The `.gdshader` text files in your Godot project ARE the source of truth — there's no JSON mirror, no `.tres` writeback, no schema. Edit a file here, save, and Godot sees the change on its next focus.

The surface ships three things on one page:

- A **CodeMirror editor** with GDShader syntax highlighting on the left.
- A **live WebGL preview** that re-translates your source on every keystroke (150ms debounce) and renders it on a canvas with auto-generated uniform controls.
- A **cross-system usages panel** that lists every `.tres` and `.tscn` that references this shader, with clickable jumps back to the source.

## The flow

1. Click into [Shaders](/shaders) from the sidebar (between Game codex and Assets).
2. The gallery surfaces every discovered `.gdshader` as a card or row — filter by text / shader_type / folder, group by either, toggle to a row view if you prefer dense.
3. Click a card to open the editor.
4. Type — the preview canvas re-translates within ~150ms.
5. Hit :kbd[Ctrl+S] (or click **Save**) to write to disk. The watcher picks up the change and pushes an SSE event so other open Bleepforge windows refresh in lockstep.

## Authoring affordances

- **+ New shader** in the list header opens a modal with a folder picker, filename, and shader_type select. The server-side template populates a minimal working shader for the picked type.
- **Right-click on any card or row**: Open · Duplicate · Delete.
- **Duplicate** prompts for a new name and saves your in-progress editor contents (not the on-disk version) — your unsaved tweaks duplicate as expected.
- **Delete** surfaces the current usage count in the confirmation so you know what's about to dangle.

## What's covered in the rest of this category

- [The editor, save, and external changes](/help/shaders/editor-and-saves) — CodeMirror behavior, dirty indicator, what the external-change banner means.
- [The live preview canvas](/help/shaders/live-preview) — 2-pass compositing, swapping the bound TEXTURE, the default UV-grid pattern.
- [Auto-generated uniform controls](/help/shaders/uniform-controls) — sliders / color pickers / vectors from your `hint_range` and `source_color` annotations.
- [Translator subset reference](/help/shaders/translator-subset) — what GDShader features the live preview supports.
- [When the live preview says unsupported](/help/shaders/unsupported-pane) — failure modes catalog with workarounds.
- [Helper functions and main-scope built-ins](/help/shaders/helper-functions) — the one gotcha worth knowing about.
